en.extract:
The ivory-backed woodswallow (Artamus monachus) is a species of bird in the family Artamidae. It is endemic to Sulawesi, Indonesia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''ivory-backed woodswallow''' ('''''Artamus monachus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Artamidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Sulawesi]], [[Indonesia]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
